Notice Of Motion And Motion For Additional Time To Conduct Pltf Leta Nichols' Deposition, With Proof Of Service; Memo Of P&A Filed In Support; Declaration Of Robert J. Kwant Filed In Support; Exhibit "G" Through Exhibit "Z" To The Declaration Of Robert J. Kwant Filed In Support
Off Calendar Jun-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert J. Kwant for defendant and moving party: Hill Brohters Chemical Company
Carlos J. Guzman for plaintiff and opposing party: Leta Nichols

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: The Court has read and considered the untimely opposition to the motion filed by counsel for plaintiff. Defendant's motion for additional time to conduct the deposition of Leta Nichols is granted. Plaintiff Leta Nichols may be deposed for an additional 16 hours. The Court finds good cause to permit additional time for deposition based upon the familiarity of the witness with the occupational history of the decedent. The deposition shall resume at a time mutually convenient to the parties in Sacramento, California. Counsel for the respective defendants should confer to ensure that the person designated as having primary responsibility for the examination of the witness does not inquire into matters of marginal significance that do not meaningfully test the credibility of the plaintiff. In addition, the Court notes that counsel for plaintiff should refrain from repeatedly reminding defendants of specified provisions of the general orders or commenting in such a manner that impedes efforts at informal resolution of disputed matters. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
